text:
### 2.7


For help on preparing a Parenting Plan, see –


(1) Cafcass ‘Putting Your Children First: A Guide for Separated Parents’ (see also paragraph 4 below);


(2) A draft of a Parenting Plan for parents or families to complete: https://www.cafcass.gov.uk/download/4365 (opens in a new tab)


(3) A draft of a Parenting Plan prepared by CAFCASS Cymru for parents or families to complete: http://gov.wales/docs/cafcass/publications/071015ParentingPlanEn.pdf (opens in a new tab) (this links to the draft plan in English) or http://gov.wales/docs/cafcass/publications/071015ParentingPlanCy.pdf (opens in a new tab) (this links to the draft plan in Welsh).


### 2.8


Publicly funded mediation and/or legal advice : If parents need access to mediation, and legal advice in support of that mediation, they may be eligible for public funding. The Legal Aid Agency (LAA) will provide funding for Mediation Information and Assessment Meetings (MIAMs) and family mediation for all those who are eligible:


(1) Where at least one party is eligible, the LAA will cover the costs of both parties to attend a MIAM to encourage any non-eligible client to find out about the benefits and suitability of mediation and other forms of non-court dispute resolution, without incurring any costs.


(2) The LAA will provide public funding for eligible parties to participate in family mediation and they may also receive some independent legal advice connected to the mediation process and where a settlement is reached can receive legal assistance to draft and issue proceedings to obtain a consent order.


(3) Parties may find out if they are likely to be eligible for legal aid at the following link: https://www.gov.uk/check-legal-aid (opens in a new tab)


(4) To find the nearest publicly funded mediation service a client can use the search at http://find-legal-advice.justice.gov.uk (opens in a new tab) . Publicly funded legal advisors can be found at: https://www.gov.uk/check-legal-aid (opens in a new tab)
